I understand the need for player retention, but I feel like the current rate for acquiring reality verges (which I will shorten to RV) may be a bit too slow. Sure, getting one daily may seem a lot, but that requires someone to log in daily- which is something that casual players may be unable to manage. As it stands, there are way too many ways to spend them on but too few to use.

A new player can progress on the main story so fast that they miss things that happen in between the main chapters. This can cause some confusion and frustration on having that specific missing lore be timegated.

I'm not asking that this mechanic to be removed. Just that, maybe just a few more sources for RVs  should be added.

Maybe like a passive '1 RVs given per week' that doesn't need to be claimed, having it regenerate like stamina.

Or refunding spent RV after finishing an event unlocked with it.

Or maybe have RVs added as bonus rewards from high difficulty content and events.

Or maybe progressing to a certain point in the endgame unlocks some of the older events for free.

Again, I understand that RV's  are meant to keep players invested, but it's hard to maintain enthusiasm for / remember things that a player is invested in when more and more story is being added that may eventually overshadow and overwhelm the older stuff.

A small example would be like how I'm torn on unlocking Lemurian stuff (Desecrate) which I really want to know more on, or the supplementary stuff for Chapter 7 (Timeworn Ramona).

It would take 10 days to unlock both, in which I surely will have progressed in the main story to be encountering more stuff I would want more elaboration on. 

I don't really want to have to rely on third-party sources like youtube and such. It would kill my desire to play the game in the first place.

That's all. Sorry for the awkward English.

Hi, wondering if anyone know what I can do or who can help me.

I played Morimens on my iPhone. When I set up the game for the first time, it logged in automatically (I assumed with my Apple account) and I played for some time, unlocking characters and doing missions. Some time ago there was an update that forced me to log in again, and this time it clearly asked me for my login details. I tried my Apple account, but that reset all progress, and I was back in the introduction. I don't know what account it made originally for my progress, or how it stored my data. 

Is there any way to find out where my progress is and get it back?
tnx
Must have been a different login info
Accounts shouldn't just nuke themselves out of existence

You want to make sure you are logging with the same login option.

Cause you may have the same email but log in with a different login option which results in a new account unless binded via email or phone.

Or were linked via the link options in the user center

My personal suggestion if you really are unsure what login option you used is to.... Try them all.

Especially if you use the same email you should hit it sooner or later
Problem is that when I first started up the game, it logged in without asking for credentials.
So whatever login or account it made for me, I have no idea how it did that.
It was not my Apple ID, since that login does not work. And I never gave any other login credentials on my iPhone.
I would also like to add I receive no emails from Morimens on any account, so I do not think my original login was based on an email at all.

So you are telling just by opening the game for the first time ever you just got tossed in with not even asking you what login to use? 

Crazy
I think you are shit outta luck unless you have the uid so Devs can check it for you but I doubt they can help you beyond that
Still suggesting to try any of these logins. It might have just made a "guest" account

For account-related issues, you'll need to contact support directly. You can log into an existing account and find the support option from the main menu or in-game — they can look into all your account details, help with unbinding, or handle other requests like that.

They don't know their original account 😔 

Aight I pass it to you Lyra.
Yeah, I am just going to assume that due to it having issues in the beginning, it made some wonky nameless account, and it's now lost.
I will leave it where it's at, if I cannot log in then I don't have to worry about playing either.
Any account works — it's just for getting in touch with support. Just log in and then head to the main menu.
Thanks for the help!
You can totally just create a new account to reach out to support — no worries!
Support will look into all possible accounts linked to you and provide the relevant assistance based on what they find.
✅「Answered」Forced login on iPhone changed/reset my account
I managed to fix it, I looked through the login options. When I selected the Qookka Games option, it told me that my login had expired and I needed to login manually.
I could not do that, because I did not have a login.
I noticed that at the bottom of the options was the Qookka logo, with no text, just as a logo among all the other login options. Clicking that logged me in as a guest.
The guest account had all my progress. It told me it was not linked to any account, and I bound it to an email address, so now the progress is saved.
For future reference, in case someone else has this issue.

✅「Answered」enemy heath bars overlaping
I did perception at the shop for free refresh, refreshed, bought a relic, exited, did a mob fight then came back to the same shop but the refresh is now 20 sigils.

The Wheel of Destiny's effect works like this: after Unleashing "Perception," the refresh is only free during that current visit to "Ashen Ruins" — it's not permanently free for "Ashen Ruins" overall. So once you leave and come back, it's no longer free.

The English description probably didn't make this clear enough — I've reported it to the relevant team, and the wording will be updated in a future update~
Thanks for the feedback.<:24_smile:1459073862544130131>
